behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing, and all missing fields are reported.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestrator + validator interaction: the intake flow must short-circuit before calling insurance/triage/scheduling modules. A unit test of only the validator could miss downstream calls still happening. Rejected System because no real OS/network/subprocess behavior is required by this behavior text; in-process module wiring is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake with missing name and insurance ID using real intake-service wiring and mocked downstream adapters; assert error lists both missing fields and assert no downstream adapter was invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates older than 150 years, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: This is pure parsing/range logic in one validation boundary with no required module handoff, I/O, or model behavior. Rejected Integration because behavior text is only local DOB rule evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven validator test for valid DOB, future DOB, DOB older than 150 years, and malformed strings; assert exact validation outcomes.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID and plan code to payer API and interprets eligible/not eligible/pending responses.
behaviors.justification: Needs service-client contract wiring: request mapping + response translation across modules. A pure unit test of mapper/decoder separately can miss mismatch at call boundary. Rejected System because real network is not required to expose this behavior-specific failure; a controlled in-process fake HTTP client/server can validate payload and mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Use insurance service with a fake payer endpoint/client that records request body and returns each status variant; assert outgoing patient ID/plan code and mapped internal eligibility state.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility is marked pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is error-propagation and fallback policy across intake + insurance modules. Simulating connection/timeouts via client exception in-process is enough to catch incorrect fallback/flow control. Rejected System because behavior text does not require proving real socket/OS timeout semantics, only unreachable handling outcome.
behaviors.test_description: Inject payer client failure (connection error/timeout exception) during intake; assert eligibility becomes pending and pipeline proceeds to later steps instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: AI symptom triage assigns high urgency for emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model clinical judgment quality; stubs would always pass and hide misclassification. This is exactly LLM/model-output-dependent behavior. Rejected Workflow because this behavior is a single agentic decision step, not multiple agentic steps chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated real triage model calls on curated emergency symptom cases and assert high-urgency classification rate/threshold meets acceptance criteria.

behaviors.description: Symptom triage returns a clinician-reviewable natural-language explanation, not only a numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Behavior quality depends on real model-generated language adequacy/readability; deterministic stubs can hide failures where output is empty, incoherent, or non-explanatory. Rejected Integration because checking only field presence is weaker than the stated clinician-reviewable requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Call real triage model on representative symptom inputs; assert response includes substantive natural-language rationale meeting rubric checks (not just numeric label).

behaviors.description: Appointment is scheduled in first available slot matching urgency policy (high same-day, medium within 48 hours).
behaviors.justification: Needs scheduling policy + calendar repository wiring with real query/update behavior in local DB; failure appears at module handoff/state level, not single-function logic alone. Rejected System because no required real external network/subprocess behavior is stated.
behaviors.test_description: With seeded local calendar DB, run scheduler for high and medium urgency requests; assert selected slot is earliest valid one and falls within policy windows.

behaviors.description: Scheduling does not double-book a slot under concurrent patient processing.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is concurrency + transactional integrity across scheduler and shared calendar DB modules. Unit tests miss race conditions. Rejected System because local concurrent workers against local DB are enough; no external OS/network/subprocess dependency is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Execute two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same candidate slot against the real scheduling stack and local shared DB; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate rows/overlap exists.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Primarily template/composition logic; can be validated at one function/object boundary with deterministic inputs. Rejected Integration because behavior text does not require validating transport, delivery channel, or external dependencies.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation message builder with fixture input for each visit type and assert required fields and matching preparation instructions are present.

behaviors.description: Full intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes automatically for a real patient submission.
behaviors.justification: End-to-end completion includes a real AI triage step whose non-deterministic output can break downstream parsing/routing; stubbing triage would hide real failures. Also spans multiple module boundaries. Rejected Workflow because behavior text specifies one agentic step (triage) inside a larger pipeline, not multiple agentic steps in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake with production-like wiring, real triage model, and real local persistence; submit a real-format patient payload and assert pipeline reaches confirmation without manual intervention.
